expectations.text: SPEC.md exists at the root of the output directory
expectations.evidence: no SPEC.md found anywhere under outputs/

expectations.text: no leftover scratch files (_spec.txt, _spec.html, _toc.md, _spec_part_*.md, _sections_index.md) in the output directory
SPEC.md contains a `## Lexical Elements (Tokens)` section
the six top-level sections appear in this exact order: Overview, Lexical Elements (Tokens), Structure (Grammar), Semantics, Examples, Appendix
expectations.evidence: missing sections: ['## Overview', '## Lexical Elements (Tokens)', '## Structure (Grammar)', '## Semantics', '## Examples', '## Appendix']

expectations.text: the string literal description covers the escape sequences \", \\, \/, \b, \f, \n, \r, \t, and \uXXXX
the number literal description covers optional leading minus, integer part, fractional part, and exponent (e/E with optional sign)
true, false, and null are documented (under Keywords/Reserved Words or as their own token classes)
the structural tokens { } [ ] : , are each documented with their role
grammar productions for object, array, and member (or value/element) appear in EBNF or ABNF
the Examples section contains at least three distinct example documents (minimal/typical/complex or equivalent)
every example under ## Examples is syntactically valid JSON (parses with json.loads)
I-JSON, security considerations, and the obsolete profiles are NOT extracted as part of the spec — user explicitly excluded them
